channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ongoing Tensions and Military Strikes in the Middle East on March 14, 2026
item.description: As of March 14, 2026, the Middle East is experiencing escalating tensions with active military engagements. Recent reports indicate a series of airstrikes carried out by the United States targeting st...

item.title: Understanding South Africa's Dependence on Imported Fuel and Its Economic Impact
item.description: South Africa’s domestic fuel prices are heavily influenced by international oil markets and the value of the Rand against the US Dollar, as most crude oil and refined petroleum products are imported. ...

item.title: Nairobi Businesswoman Supports Kayole Teen with Free Make-Up Training After Father Abandons Her
item.description: Elizabeth Wanjiku Nzisa, a young girl from Kayole, Nairobi, has shown remarkable courage after losing both her parents—her mother during childbirth and her father who disappeared shortly afterwards. A...

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Upholds Medicaid Exclusion on Gender-Affirming Surgeries for Adults
item.description: A recent ruling by the 4th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals has upheld a West Virginia law that excludes coverage for gender-affirming surgeries for transgender adults under Medicaid. The unanimous decis...

item.title: Kerala Government Reconsiders and Aligns with Sabarimala Tradition on Women’s Entry
item.description: The Left Democratic Front (LDF) government in Kerala has made a notable policy reversal regarding the Sabarimala temple issue. On March 14, 2026, the state decided to support a Supreme Court affidavit...

item.title: Tanzanian Women Leading in Politics, Agriculture, and Community Development
item.description: On March 8, 2026, Tanzania celebrated International Women's Day with widespread events showing the increasing role of women in leadership, politics, and business. The country is moving from seeing wom...

item.title: Experts Raise Concerns Over Nigeria’s GMO Regulation Transparency
item.description: Two well-known Nigerian experts, environmental activist Nnimmo Bassey and public health scientist Joyce Brown, don raise alarm say National Biosafety Management Agency (NBMA) no dey transparent for ho...

item.title: John Lennon's Piano Sells for Record $3.3 Million at Beatles Memorabilia Auction
item.description: A piano once used by John Lennon to compose several iconic songs for The Beatles, including 'Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds,' 'A Day In The Life,' and 'Being For The Benefit Of Mr Kite!,' has sold for ...

item.title: Uncertainty surrounds role of new national security and intelligence adviser

item.title: Netflix documentary examines the Murdoch family's influence and the future of News Corp

item.title: Northern Youths Ask FG to Maintain Tantita Pipeline Security Contract to Boost Employment and Stability
item.description: The Northern Ethnic Youths Group Assembly (NEYGA) don advise President Bola Ahmed Tinubu make e no cancel the pipeline surveillance contract wey Tantita Security Services Nigeria Limited (TSSNL) dey h...

item.title: Iran assures Strait of Hormuz remains open amid West Asia tensions; Saudi oil tanker reaches India
item.description: Tensions in West Asia have escalated following joint military operations by Israel and the United States against Iran that began on February 28. The conflict has already resulted in significant casual...

item.title: New book by Tom Bower alleges Queen Camilla claimed Meghan Markle had brainwashed Prince Harry amid royal family tensions
item.description: A new book by author Tom Bower, serialised in The Times, details the deepening rift within the British Royal Family following Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's marriage. Tensions reportedly escalated s...

item.description: The race for the 2025/2026 Tanzania Mainland Premier League title is heating up, with Simba SC and Azam FC scoring important away wins that increase pressure on the defending champions, Young Africans...

item.title: Senior Maoist Leader Claims Arrest, Not Voluntary Surrender
item.description: A senior Maoist leader has publicly stated that he did not surrender voluntarily to the authorities but was instead arrested. This clarification comes amid conflicting reports that suggested his surre...

item.title: Inspector General Kanja Calls for Stronger Police Accountability and Service Improvements at County Commanders' Conference

item.title: Akwa Ibom High Court Acquits Judiciary Accountant of N20 Million Theft Allegations Due to Insufficient Evidence
item.description: For nearly six years, Iniobong Essien, an Accountant II with the Akwa Ibom State Judiciary, dey face trial for alleged conspiracy and stealing of N20 million wey dem withdraw from Zenith Bank for offi...

item.title: CRDB Bank Launches Promo Sending 10 Customers to FIFA World Cup in US and Canada

item.title: Envoi Allen, notable racehorse, dies at Cheltenham Festival after final race
item.description: During the 2026 Cheltenham Festival, tragedy struck as the 12-year-old racehorse Envoi Allen collapsed and died following his ninth-place finish in the Boodles Cheltenham Gold Cup. This event marks th...
